name: Tamale Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 cups cooked pinto or kidney beans
1/2 cup fresh, frozen, or canned yellow corn, optional
recipeInstructions:
CRUST
3 cups boiling water
1 cup cornmeal — coarsely ground
OR
1 1/2 cups regular cornmeal
1 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon chili powder
1/4 cup grated cheddar cheese — for topping
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Saute onion and garlic in oil. When nearly soft, add chili powder and cumin and crush garlic with a fork. Continue to cook and stir until the spices are fragrant. Meanwhile, mash beans and mix them with the tomato paste into the onion mixture, along with the other filling ingredients. When hot, adjust seasonings and set aside.
Stir cornmeal slowly into boiling water (if using regular cornmeal instead of coarse, first stir it into 1 cup cold water, then into 2 cups boiling water). Cook and stir until thick, adding salt and chili powder once the mixture comes to a boil.
Grease an 8x8-inch pan and spread 2/3 of the cornmeal mixture over the bottom and sides. Pour the bean mixture into this crust. Drop and spread the remaining third of the cornmeal on top (don't worry if it doesn't cover completely). Sprinkle the top with grated cheddar cheese and cook for 30 minutes.
The recipe says it serves four -- generously.
